body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial (yes for two distinct session rows + independent arcs; partial for stable per-session PID binding)
applicable: false — driver_gap, no recording. The pi interactive driver cannot launch two concurrent same-cwd sessions, so the behavior cannot be elicited for capture; the spec is committed as the documented target.

## Reasoning

`multiple-sessions-same-cwd` (scenario-meanings.md): two instances of the SAME agent launched in one working directory must appear as two distinct session rows, each with its own session_id and PID, transitioning state independently with no flapping and no PID cross-contamination. Primitive exercised = PID-based session isolation. Distinct from `session-resume` (one session_id reused across PID lifetimes) and from `multiple-agents-same-workspace` (different agent types).

**agent_supports: yes.** Pi launches as an independent REPL per invocation; two `pi` processes can run in the same cwd as-shipped. Each mints its own session UUID and writes a distinct transcript at `~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<uuid>.jsonl` — the path is keyed by cwd-slug + timestamp + UUID, so two sessions in one cwd produce two distinct transcript files (capabilities.json: session_id_assignment:false — pi mints its own UUID, so a recipe could not pre-pin the two ids).

**irrlicht_observes: partial.** Two strands:
- TWO DISTINCT ROWS + INDEPENDENT ARCS (yes). Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}} (agent.go). The fswatcher picks up two new transcript files (one per UUID) → two SessionState rows, and the JSONL tailer instantiates a FRESH stateful Parser{} per file, so the two sessions never share parser state — a turn on one transcript only updates that session's metrics. Independent working→ready arcs are observed cleanly.
- STABLE PER-SESSION PID BINDING (partial — this is the load-bearing weakness). pi's DiscoverPID = processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD("pi", cwd, disambiguate) (pid.go) — it lists all `pi` PIDs (pgrep -x), narrows by matching the process cwd to the session's working directory, then on multiple matches falls back to the `disambiguate` callback (default: highest PID). For two pi processes in the SAME cwd, BOTH PIDs survive the cwd narrowing, so the two same-cwd sessions resolve against the SAME candidate set and the daemon cannot deterministically bind each session to its OWN PID by cwd alone. This is materially weaker than codex (DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter — binds by which process holds each rollout file open, so two same-cwd codex sessions are cleanly disambiguated by file ownership) and claudecode (metadata-first ~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json lookup + strict unambiguous-or-zero cwd fallback hardened in #109/#169). pi has neither a per-transcript-writer probe nor a per-PID metadata file, so the scenario's central 'PID-to-session binding is stable and does not cross-contaminate' invariant is NOT guaranteed for pi the way it is for the other two adapters.

## Why this cell is applicable:false (driver_gap, not a verdict downgrade)

The blocking issue is upstream of the daemon: the pi interactive driver (`scripts/drive-pi-interactive.sh`) is strictly single-session. It supports only send / slash / wait_turn / interrupt / sleep, runs ONE tmux session, resolves ONE transcript, and tracks ONE EXPECTED_TURNS counter. It has no `start_session` case label and no per-step {"session":N} routing. The claudecode and codex recipes for THIS scenario both require `start_session` + {"session":N} (their drivers gained a per-slot session model: step_start_session, slot allocation, claimed-path exclusion). The pi driver has none of that, so the two-concurrent-same-cwd-sessions condition cannot be staged for a recording. Per the implement pipeline this is a driver_gap (C9 family): a developer task to extend the pi driver, out of scope for the cell. expected.jsonl is committed as the documented target (mirroring the codex/claudecode spec shape, anchored on working transitions to dodge pi's born-ready proc-<PID> placeholder); by_adapter.pi is marked applicable:false with a scope_note; no recording is produced and no retry is attempted.

## Status of this cell

Verdict: agent_supports yes, irrlicht_observes partial (confidence 0.78). Recording is blocked by the single-session pi driver (driver_gap), not by the verdict. The two next actions to finish this cell are BOTH developer tasks: (1) extend drive-pi-interactive.sh with a start_session / per-slot session model (as claudecode/codex have); and (2) decide whether pi's cwd-only DiscoverPID needs hardening to guarantee the stable per-session PID binding the scenario asserts (the partial in irrlicht_observes). Re-author + record after (1) lands; (2) gates whether the recording would actually satisfy the 'no PID cross-contamination' assertion.
caveats: DRIVER GAP: drive-pi-interactive.sh is single-session (send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep only — no start_session, no {"session":N} targeting). It cannot launch two concurrent same-cwd pi sessions, so this cell cannot be recorded until the driver gains a per-slot session model like claudecode's/codex's. This is the reason for applicable:false.

PID-BINDING SEAM (irrlicht_observes:partial): pi's DiscoverPID = DiscoverPIDByCWD — cwd-only narrowing with a highest-PID disambiguate fallback. Two pi processes in the SAME cwd both survive the narrowing, so the daemon cannot deterministically bind each session to its own PID by cwd alone (no per-transcript-writer probe like codex, no per-PID metadata file like claudecode). The scenario's 'stable, non-cross-contaminating PID binding' invariant is not guaranteed for pi.

pi mints its own session UUID (session_id_assignment:false), so — like codex and unlike claudecode — a recipe cannot pre-pin the two ids; the spec asserts two DISTINCT sessions via new_session/same_session_as relations.

Every expected.jsonl phase anchors on a working transition, never a ready: pi's process scanner (ExactName{pi}, pgrep -x) emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready before the transcript is discovered; a ready-anchored new_session phase would greedily bind to that placeholder.

sources.note: multiple-sessions-same-cwd: two instances of the same agent in one cwd → two distinct session_id+PID rows, independent transitions, stable non-cross-contaminating PID binding; primitive = PID-based session isolation; distinct from session-resume and multiple-agents-same-workspace.

DRIVER GAP root cause: single-session driver. case labels = send|slash|wait_turn|interrupt|sleep only; one tmux SESSION, one TRANSCRIPT, one EXPECTED_TURNS counter; no start_session, no {"session":N} routing. Cannot stage two concurrent same-cwd pi sessions.

Contrast: the codex driver HAS step_start_session + slot allocation + per-step {"session":N} routing with claimed-path exclusion. This is the concurrent-second-session primitive the pi driver lacks; the codex/claudecode recipes for this scenario depend on it.

Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}} — a fresh stateful parser per transcript file means two pi sessions in one cwd do not share parser state (independent arcs are observable). Process.Match = ExactName{pi} (pgrep -x).

DiscoverPID delegates to processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD("pi", cwd, disambiguate): cwd-only narrowing. Weaker than codex's per-rollout-file-writer binding and claudecode's metadata-first lookup; two same-cwd pi processes both survive the narrowing → cannot be cleanly bound to distinct PIDs by cwd alone (irrlicht_observes:partial).

DiscoverPIDByCWD → narrowByCWD: lists matching PIDs, filters by cwd, and on multiple matches falls back to disambiguate (default highest PID). No per-process metadata and no per-file-writer probe, so two same-cwd processes are indistinguishable by this path alone.
headless_mode:true (scenario requires it); session_id_assignment:false (pi mints its own UUID — recipe cannot pre-pin the two ids); subagents:false, permission_hooks:false.

Sibling codex cell: recorded (yes/yes) via the codex driver's start_session model. Same spec shape; the pi spec is adapted from it. The pi cell is blocked only by the pi driver lacking that step model.
